revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Created page with ''''Keta Shrine''' (竈山神社, ''Keta taisha'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Hakui, Ishikawa]] Prefecture, Japan. It was formerly an national shrine of the firs...'
revision.text:
'''Keta Shrine''' (竈山神社, ''Keta taisha'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Hakui, Ishikawa]] Prefecture, Japan. It was formerly an national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is [[Ōkuninushi]]. It was the former [[ichinomiya]] of [[Noto Province]].
The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3.
==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Keta Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[ja:気多大社]] [[zh:氣多大社]]

[[File:Keta-taisha haiden.JPG|thumb|Keta Taisha worship hall]]'''Keta Shrine''' (竈山神社, ''Keta taisha'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Hakui, Ishikawa]] Prefecture, Japan. It was formerly an national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is [[Ōkuninushi]]. It was the former [[ichinomiya]] of [[Noto Province]].
The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3.
==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]]
==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | width = 250 | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | type = | dedication = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded = pre-[[Nara period]] | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | phone = | website = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former [[ichinomiya]] of [[Noto Province]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the dmi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]]or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Keta Taisha]][[Category:kk]] It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Keta Taisha]][[Category:Pages and Categories from {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Keta Taisha]][[en:Keta Taisha]][[Category:Pages and The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[en:Keta Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[en:Keta It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[en:Keta {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[en:Keta {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[en:Keta {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[en:Keta {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[en:Keta {{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Keta Taisha]][[Category:qq]] revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Keta Taisha]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Keta Taisha]] [[en:Keta Taisha]]

気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Keta Taisha]] [[en:Keta Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Keta Taisha]] [[en:Keta Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = Keta-taisha haiden.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36.9260|136.7675|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | founded_by = | established = pre-[[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' by [[Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Keta Taisha]] [[en:Keta Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

気多大社 | image = File:Keta-taisha keidai.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.926|N|136.768|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = pre-[[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Hakui|qid=Q822585}} }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' by [[:en:Otomo no Yakamochi|Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Keta Taisha]] [[en:Keta Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

気多大社 | image = File:Keta-taisha keidai.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.926|N|136.768|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = pre-[[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Hakui|qid=Q822585}} }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Hakui, Ishikawa|Hakui]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' by [[:en:Otomo no Yakamochi|Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] }} [[en:Keta Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q11129340}}

It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]]. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] to subdue the inhabitants of [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]] during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] or 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' by [[:en:Otomo no Yakamochi|Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Keta Taisha]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] {{wikidata link|Q11129340}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Five of the shrine's structures have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] [[Category:11 commons]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Keta Taisha]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] {{wikidata link|Q11129340}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

気多大社 | image = File:Keta-taisha keidai.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.926|N|136.768|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = pre-{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Hakui|WD=Q822585}} }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Hakui, Ishikawa|en|Hakui, Ishikawa|WD=Q822585|lt=Hakui}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province}}. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo}} to subdue the inhabitants of {{ill|Noto Peninsula|en|Noto Peninsula|WD=Q691151|lt=Noto Peninsula}} during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}} or 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' by [[:en:Otomo no Yakamochi|Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] [[Category:11 commons]] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Keta Taisha]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] {{wikidata link|Q11129340}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior First Rank]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Second Rank]] [[Category:Junior Second Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

Five of the shrine's structures have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Keta Taisha]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] {{wikidata link|Q11129340}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior First Rank]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Second Rank]] [[Category:Junior Second Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Keta Taisha]] {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} [[ja:気多大社]] [[simple:Keta Shrine]] [[zh:氣多大社]] {{wikidata link|Q11129340}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior First Rank]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Second Rank]] [[Category:Junior Second Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Keta Jinja}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Shrine
気多大社 | image = File:Keta-taisha keidai.JPG | caption = ''Haiden'' of Keta Taisha | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.926|N|136.768|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | founded_by = | established = pre-{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | date_destroyed = | location = Hakui-shi, Ishikawa-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Hakui|qid=Q822585|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}} }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Shrine'''|気多大社|Keta Taisha}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Hakui, Ishikawa|en|Hakui, Ishikawa|qid=Q822585|lt=Hakui|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was the former ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|qid=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}. The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined is [[Ōkuninushi]]. The shrine's main festival is held annually on April 3. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed on the location where Ōkuninushi landed with 300 of his folders from {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}} to subdue the inhabitants of {{ill|Noto Peninsula|en|Noto Peninsula|qid=Q691151|lt=Noto Peninsula|1=Noto Peninsula}} during the reign of the demi-legendary 8th {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}} or 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. The shrine is first mentioned in history in a poem in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' by [[:en:Otomo no Yakamochi|Otomo no Yakamochi]] dated 748 AD. It is also mentioned in the 768 AD ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', and by the 859 AD ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', it had been accorded 1st Court Rank. The shrine was patronised by the [[Maeda clan]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and a number of its structures date from this time. Under the pre-World War II [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the shrine was classified as a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha''). Five of the shrine's structures have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}: * [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']], built in 1787 * [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']], built in 1653 * Gate, built in 1583 * sub-shrine Hakusan Jinja ''Haiden'' * sub-shrine Wakamiya Jinja ''Haiden'' ==See also== *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==External links== *[http://www.keta.jp Official website] [[Category|Keta-taisha}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{Commons category|Keta-taisha}} {{wikidata link|Q11129340|co|commons:Category:Keta-taisha|en|Keta Taisha|ja|気多大社|simple|Keta Shrine|zh|氣多大社}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hakui, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior First Rank]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Second Rank]] [[Category:Junior Second Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
